name: Chunky Chocolate Pecan Pie
recipeIngredient: 1/3 cup Unsalted butter; chilled and cut into pieces
recipeInstructions:
Pastry:
1. Mix together 1 1/4 cups flour, butter, sugar and salt until crumbly.
2. Add ice water by spoonfuls while mixing on low speed until a dough forms.
3. Remove dough from bowl and place on a lightly floured surface.
4. Knead into a smooth dough ball. Flatten into a disk.
5. Cover with plastic wrap and chill for a few hours.
6. When chilled, roll out into a 15-inch circle about 1/8 inch thick.
7. Place in a 9-inch tart pan with removable bottom.
8. Press into the bottom and sides of the pan. Trim off excess dough leaving a 1-inch overhang.
9. Turn inside and crimp the edge.
10. Cover loosely and chill for 30 minutes.
Filling:
11. Preheat oven to 325 degrees.
12. Heat butter and sugar in a saucepan over medium-high heat, stirring constantly until melted.
13. Pour into a mixing bowl and add half the chocolate chips and all the unsweetened chocolate.
14. Stir until chocolate has melted.
15. Add dark corn syrup, vanilla and salt. Beat in eggs one at a time using an electric mixer on medium speed.
16. Place pastry shell on a baking sheet.
17. Evenly sprinkle pecan halves over the bottom of the pastry.
18. Sprinkle remaining 2 ounces chocolate chips over the pecans.
19. Slowly pour batter over all.
20. Bake pie for 1 hour at 325 degrees.
21. Reduce temperature to 300 degrees and bake another 40 minutes.
22. Remove pie from oven and cool to room temperature before chilling for 1 hour before cutting.
